2023.diseaseSubtitle: Chemotaxis to TME · ADC and lactate sensing
2023.title: SYPHU-China 2023 Project
2023.description: The 2023 project focused on HER2-positive solid tumours, combining motility circuits with ADC-style delivery and synthetic parts such as eTlpC and strong promoters.

2024.diseaseSubtitle: L. reuteri skin targeting · dynamic AMP release
2024.title: SYPHU-China 2024 Project
2024.description: Engineered Lactobacillus reuteri DSM17938 delivers a long-acting skin-microenvironment therapy with inducible antimicrobial peptides, aiming to reduce relapse versus conventional antifungal approaches.

2025.diseaseSubtitle: E. coli Nissle 1917 targets TME · ATRA and resistance reversal
2025.title: SYPHU-China 2025 Project
2025.description: Using engineered E. coli Nissle 1917, the team enables chemotaxis toward lactate-rich tumour microenvironments and in situ synthesis of all‑trans retinoic acid, with modelling and experiments supporting improved survival endpoints in advanced liver cancer.

team.title: Our Team
team.rosterIntroAfter: ; bios are shortened for readability. Full English statements are on the Wiki.
team.workResearchDesc: Frontier work in synthetic biology, genetic engineering, and microbiology.
team.workLabDesc: Experimental data and validation from our projects.

recruit.heroLead: iGEM is more than wet lab and modelling—it is interdisciplinary teamwork and global exchange. Below are common team roles; reach us via the form at the bottom.
recruit.expectFoot: Team posts often stress fit, transferable skills, and teamwork—the above is for reference; the current cycle’s interview notice prevails.
recruit.nextBody: Typical flow: follow the team’s WeChat / announcements → submit interest or join interviews → training and roles. Exact dates and formats are published yearly by the leads; you can leave your track and contact in the form below.
recruit.contactHint: Please include year/major, preferred track, availability, and a reachable email.

wet.title: Wet lab
wet.short: Hands-on lab work—the physical core of the synthetic biology project.
points: Cloning, culture, transformation, expression, gels/sequencing, phenotypes; hand data to dry lab.
You: bio/chem/pharmacy basics, careful hands, safety and records; fixed lab hours; tolerate iteration.
Beginners welcome if motivated: shared SOP training; responsibility and commitment matter most.

dry.title: Dry lab
dry.short: Modelling, simulation, data analysis, and computational support.
points: Dynamics/flux models, stats and plots, parameter fitting and sensitivity; small scripts or pipelines.
You: calculus + coding (Python/MATLAB/R), clear hypotheses, figures and text ready for Wiki.
Math, CS, stats, pharmaceutical engineering (computational) backgrounds welcome; English reading matters.

hp.title: Human Practices (HP)
hp.short: Taking the project beyond the lab: society, ethics, education, outreach.
points: Interviews, surveys, outreach, policy/ethics, hospital or industry contacts; materials and narratives.
You: communication and writing; run interviews and events; English helps for judging materials.
Public health, clinical pharmacy, management, journalism, English, etc.—empathy and execution.

wiki.title: Wiki / IT
wiki.short: Official Wiki site, information architecture, and technical implementation.
points: IA, migration, styles, Git collaboration, pre-deadline integration; coordinate timing with art and subteams.
You: HTML/CSS basics or willing to learn; read English docs; version control; crunch-time edits.
Division from pure design: Wiki focuses on site and docs; art supplies major visuals.

art.title: Art & design
art.short: Visual identity, infographics, presentation and promo materials.
points: Branding, posters, handouts, infographics, deck visuals, short video or motion (as assigned).
You: PS/AI/ID or similar; layout and colour; PR/AE/Blender is a plus.
Iterate toward Wiki and presentation deadlines; portfolios (coursework or competitions) help matching.

commonExpect: Align with team goals; attend meetings; high commitment from summer to competition.
Report progress honestly; follow lab safety, ethics, and university rules.
English: read Wiki and papers; speaking and defence improve during prep.
All years welcome—many teams mentor juniors from basic roles.

labIntro.title: SYPHU · Laboratories & research environment
labIntro.lead: SYPHU hosts a national pharmaceutical experimental teaching centre (established 2001; national status 2007) with multiple sub-centres, supporting wet lab and interdisciplinary work for teams like SYPHU-China iGEM.
labIntro.centerLabel: National experimental teaching centre (pharmacy) — official site

partnership.heroLead: We welcome exchanges with iGEM teams worldwide and partnerships with industry, translating ideas from class and lab into the real world.
partnership.inviteTitle: Invitation to the global iGEM community
partnership.inviteP1: iGEM is built on open collaboration: we want long-term links with teams worldwide—wet and dry lab, Human Practices and outreach, Wiki and judging prep, and joint activities within the rules.
partnership.inviteP2: For guest talks, joint outreach, or friendly reviews before judging, use the form at the bottom and include your school/team and preferred format.
partnership.addrShenyang: 103 Wenhua Road, Shenhe District, Shenyang, Liaoning, China
partnership.addrBenxi: 26 Huatuo Street, Benxi High-tech Zone, Liaoning · 117004 (south campus exists; day-to-day arrangements follow the school).

hospitals.title: Partnering with 26 leading hospitals as teaching hospitals
hospitals.line: Signing and inauguration at the Shenyang campus to expand clinical teaching and medicine–education collaboration.

belgium.title: Belgian mission visits Benxi · anti-aging cooperation
belgium.line: Gao Mingyu joined talks with Belgian partners on biomedicine and anti-aging.

aging.title: Anti-aging institute · symposium on aging and degenerative disease research
aging.line: Experts from the China National Center for Biotechnology Development and provincial S&T department visited for a focused discussion.

nmpa.title: New milestone · NMPA regulatory science innovation research base approved
nmpa.line: Small-molecule drugs; sole university awardee in the Northeast three provinces and Inner Mongolia cluster.

mps2025.title: 2025 International Microphysiological Systems Conference · 8th organoids & organ-on-a-chip symposium
mps2025.line: Co-hosted by Southeast University, Jiangsu Provincial People’s Hospital, SYPHU, Columbia University, USA, and others; experts from many countries attended.

partnership.sponsorLead: Synthetic biology is moving from papers to products. We work with biopharma, synbio, diagnostics, and green manufacturing—supplies, characterisation, validation, outreach, and compliant co-branding.
partnership.philosophy1: Openness & sharing: the Registry, peer support, and the global community are core to iGEM.
partnership.philosophy2: Safety & responsibility: from bench to public—biosafety, ethics, responsible innovation.
partnership.philosophy3: Human Practices: projects grounded in real-world social, legal, educational, and stakeholder contexts.
partnership.philosophy4: From research to impact: strong Wikis, reproducible experiments, and translation or entrepreneurship (including related competition tracks).
partnership.competitionNote: Definitions and rules: see competition.igem.org and the handbook for the current year.
partnership.startupsLead: Figures below are official static assets from startups.igem.org, highlighting global alumni startup scale and geography.
partnership.startupsBlurb: iGEM Startups connects competition projects with mentoring and networks; the figure below summarises scale and trends.

partnership.coop1d: Strains, experimental design, analytics, safety and QC—discussion and collaboration.
partnership.coop2d: Wiki, judging, open days, social—joint visibility within iGEM rules and school policy.
partnership.coop3d: Mentorship and talent exchange; details subject to written agreements and the current handbook.

teamPi.name: Prof. Xianpu Ni
teamPi.affiliation: School of Life Science and Biopharmaceutics, SYPHU
teamPi.research: Prof. Ni works on microbial drug metabolic engineering, biosynthetic pathways and mechanisms of microbial drugs, and synthetic biology for live biotherapeutics. He has led projects funded by the NSFC, Liaoning Provincial NSF, and the Provincial Department of Education, and published in journals such as Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology and Microbial Cell Factories. He was selected for Liaoning provincial talent programmes.
teamPi.igemSupport: He champions SYPHU’s iGEM participation and synthetic-biology training, coordinates related activities on campus, and encourages students to compete and collaborate with teams worldwide.
